metadata.title: States PCS: Uttarakhand GK for UKPSC Exams

questions.question: Where is Uday Shankar Dance and Drama Academy located in Uttarakhand?
questions.explanation: Uday Shankar established his main cultural centre in Almora, Uttarakhand. The academy is located in Falsima near Almora. Almora is recognized as the location of the Uday Shankar Dance and Theatre Academy according to multiple sources.

questions.question: In which Uttarakhand city is the month-long Shravani Mela held every Shravan?
questions.explanation: Haridwar is a major pilgrimage site in Uttarakhand during Shravan when devotees gather for the Kanwar Yatra. The Shravani Mela is mainly associated with Deoghar in Jharkhand but Haridwar hosts significant Shravan festivities. Haridwar lies on the banks of the river Ganges and receives millions of pilgrims collecting holy water during this period. It is considered one of the primary Uttarakhand destinations for Shravan celebrations.

questions.question: What was Uttarakhand’s average literacy rate in 2011 (in %)?
questions.explanation: According to the 2011 Census of India, Uttarakhand’s overall literacy rate was 78.82%, representing the combined literacy of the population aged seven years and above. Male literacy was 87.40% and female literacy was 70.01%. This data includes both urban and rural areas of Uttarakhand and reflects an increase from the 2001 literacy rate of 71.62%.

questions.question: How did Uttarakhand’s decadal population growth rate in 2011 compare to India’s?

options.label: D
options.text: Almost equal to the national average

questions.explanation: According to Census 2011 data and official assessments, Uttarakhand’s decadal population growth rate was lower than the national average after adjusting for migration and revised counts. The all-India growth rate for 2001–2011 was approximately 17.7%, while Uttarakhand’s effective rate was considered lower for planning and policy purposes.

questions.question: Which tribe worships Mahasu (Mahashiv) in Uttarakhand?
questions.explanation: Mahasu Devta is a folk god worshipped mainly in the Western Himalayan region of Uttarakhand. The Jaunsari tribe, residing primarily in the Jaunsar-Bawar region, venerates Mahasu Devta as their chief deity. This local deity is considered a divine judge and holds cultural significance among the Jaunsaris. The Bhotiya, Jad, and Boksa tribes do not traditionally worship Mahasu.

questions.question: Which tribe calls their close relatives “Swara” in Uttarakhand?
questions.explanation: The Jaunsari tribe from Uttarakhand uses “Swara” to denote close blood relatives. They primarily reside in the Jaunsar–Bawar region of Dehradun district. Their kinship terminology and social customs, including clan-based relations and endogamous marriage practices, distinguish them from Bhotiya, Boksa, and Tharu tribes who do not use the term “Swara.”

questions.question: Which is the highest numbered National Highway in Uttarakhand?
questions.explanation: National Highway 34 (NH 34) in Uttarakhand runs from Gangotri Dham to Madhya Pradesh, covering about 1,426 km. It is the longest and highest numbered National Highway originating in the state. Other highways like NH 30 and NH 44 pass through Uttarakhand but have lower numbering.

questions.question: What is ringal primarily used for?
questions.explanation: Ringal is a small, thin mountain bamboo found in the Himalayas, especially Uttarakhand and Darjeeling. It is strong and flexible, commonly used by local communities for weaving carpets, baskets, bowls, and other household items. There is no traditional use of ringal in making bangles, blankets, or pearl necklaces.

questions.question: Who wrote the book “Garhwal: Ancient and Modern”?
questions.explanation: The book “Garhwal: Ancient and Modern” was authored by Rai Pati Ram Bahadur. It is a historical work on the Garhwal region written in English and was first published in the early 20th century. The book details the political, social, and cultural history of Garhwal, covering both ancient and modern times. Multiple library and archival records attribute the authorship to Pati Ram.

questions.question: Where is the Uttarakhand Administrative Training Academy located?
questions.explanation: The Uttarakhand Administrative Training Academy, also called Dr. Raghunandan Singh Tolia Uttarakhand Academy of Administration, is located at Ardwell Camp, Mallital, Nainital. It is the main training center for Uttarakhand state civil servants. The academy was relocated to Nainital in 1971 and has had several name changes before adopting its current name.
